core_mechanism: a perturbative unitary (similarity) transformation that block-diagonalizes an operator to decouple a distinguished low-dimensional subspace from its high-dimensional complement, yielding a reduced effective operator on that subspace computed order-by-order via a systematic series/diagram expansion
brief_reason: The abstract defines the method as a unitary transformation that decouples the low-energy and high-energy subspaces to produce an effective Hamiltonian, with a perturbative Taylor-series expansion of the rotation giving a diagram technique for high-order corrections.
